questions.question: Explain the difference between Raster Scan and Random Scan display systems.
keyPoints: Raster scan sweeps beam horizontally row by row from top to bottom
Random scan directs beam to specific points to draw lines between endpoints
Raster uses frame buffer, random scan stores line equations
Raster good for realistic images, random for line drawings

questions.question: What is a Frame Buffer? Explain its role in computer graphics systems.
keyPoints: Frame buffer is dedicated memory storing complete image to be displayed
Contains pixel values (color) for every screen location
Video controller reads frame buffer to generate display signals
Size depends on screen resolution and color depth

questions.question: List and explain the main components of a computer graphics system.
keyPoints: Input devices: Mouse, keyboard, light pen for user interaction
Output devices: Display monitors, printers, plotters for visual output
Frame buffer: Memory storing pixel data for display
Video controller: Manages refresh and display generation
CPU/GPU: Processes graphics commands and calculations

questions.question: Explain the concept of resolution and color depth in computer graphics.
keyPoints: Resolution: Total pixels (horizontal × vertical) that can be displayed
Higher resolution = more pixels = sharper images
Color depth: Number of bits per pixel determining colors available
24-bit color = 16.7 million colors, 8-bit = 256 colors

questions.question: What is double buffering? Why is it used in graphics systems?
keyPoints: Technique using two frame buffers for display
One buffer for drawing, one for current display
Eliminates visual flicker during animation
When drawing completes, buffers are swapped

questions.question: Explain the working principle of a CRT display system.
keyPoints: CRT uses electron gun to shoot beams at phosphor-coated screen
Electron beam deflected by magnetic fields to scan screen
Phosphor dots emit light when struck by electrons
Refresh rate determines how often screen is redrawn

questions.question: Differentiate between LCD and LED display technologies.
keyPoints: LCD uses liquid crystals that block/allow light
LED uses light-emitting diodes as backlight
LED displays are thinner, more energy-efficient
Both are flat-panel technologies replacing CRT

questions.question: Describe in detail the components and working of a computer graphics system with a neat block diagram.
keyPoints: Input Subsystem: Keyboard, mouse, digitizer for user input
Processing Subsystem: CPU, GPU for graphics calculations
Memory Subsystem: Frame buffer/VRAM storing pixel data
Output Subsystem: Video controller, display monitor
Video controller reads frame buffer, generates video signals
Display renders the stored image at refresh rate
Explain data flow from input to display with diagram

questions.question: Explain the different types of display technologies used in computer graphics.
keyPoints: CRT: Cathode ray tube using electron beams and phosphor
Raster Scan: Rows of pixels scanned sequentially
Random Scan: Direct line drawing between points
LCD: Liquid crystal display with backlight
LED: Light emitting diode backlight technology
Plasma: Ionized gas pixels emitting light
OLED: Organic LEDs for flexible displays
Compare advantages and limitations of each

questions.question: Discuss the graphics pipeline in computer graphics. Explain each stage in detail.
keyPoints: Modeling: Create 3D objects using geometric primitives
Geometry Processing: Transform, clip, shade objects
Rasterization: Convert primitives to pixel fragments
Fragment Processing: Apply textures, colors, lighting
Display: Output final 2D image to screen
Explain how each stage processes and passes data
Pipeline enables efficient real-time rendering

questions.question: Explain various input and output devices used in computer graphics systems.
keyPoints: Input: Mouse (pointing), Keyboard (text), Joystick (gaming), Light Pen (direct selection), Digitizer (vectorization), Touch Panel (direct interaction), Trackball (rotation)
Output: CRT Monitor (traditional), LCD/LED (flat panel), Printers (Dot matrix, Inkjet, Laser), Plotters (vector output)
Explain working principles of each device
Applications in different graphics scenarios
